w20: Compute-optimized server featuring 112 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 448 GB RAM for high-throughput, multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
c150: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-112: A compute-optimized server with 112 vCPUs and 448 GB RAM, designed for high-performance multi-threaded workloads.
c200: The Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-112 is a compute-optimized instance featuring 112 vCPUs and 448 GB of RAM. It utilizes AMD EPYC 7B13 processors to deliver strong multi-core performance for demanding tasks.
w150: The Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-112 is a compute-optimized server instance built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 112 dedicated vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 7B13 processors, organized into 56 physical cores with two threads per core. The system provides 448 GB of RAM, resulting in 4.0 GB of memory per core. This configuration includes 448 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets including AES, FMA, and BMI2 for cryptographic and integer operations. No GPU or local storage is bundled with this instance. Benchmark results demonstrate top-tier multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth, particularly in physics simulations, ray tracing, and encryption tasks. The architecture is optimized for high-throughput environments, making it suitable for database operations, large-scale CI/CD pipelines, static web serving, and intensive ETL processes where consistent multi-threaded compute performance is required.
